mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment above 7.5% signals labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence declines.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and labor demand signal weak economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth during late-cycle expansions drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings benefit from sustained demand.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high-GDP periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer prices and housing starts signal deflationary risks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as central banks delay rate hikes.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by rising federal funds rates and declining money supply growth produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 135,000 jobs signals robust labor market conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-PAYEMS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
